examples: He is the only doctor for miles.
The only people in the stadium were the fans: no players, coaches, or officials.

definitions.text: Without sibling; without a sibling of the same gender.
examples: He is their only son, in fact, an only child.

definitions.text: Without others or anything further; exclusively.
examples: My heart is hers, and hers only.
The cat only sat on the mat. It didn't scratch it.
If there were only one more ticket!

definitions.text: Introduces a clause with negative polarity, often one which upsets the listener's expectations. Under the condition that; but. (clarification of this definition is needed)
examples: You're welcome to borrow my bicycle, only please take care of it.
I would enjoy running, only I have this broken leg.

definitions.text: Under the condition that; but. (clarification of this definition is needed)
examples: You're welcome to borrow my bicycle, only please take care of it.

definitions.text: But, except; but in fact. (clarification of this definition is needed)
examples: I would enjoy running, only I have this broken leg.
She would get good results only she gets nervous.

definitions.text: Placed at the end of a sentence to emphasize that something is trivial, small, undemanding or deserving of belittlement.
examples: Just need to replace the filter only.
